content:
# Exodus Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~34r–34v (PDF pp. 66–67); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 46–47 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_006_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_006_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_006_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_006_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Now you shall see what I will do to Par'oh, for by a strong hand he will send them out, and by a strong hand he will drive them out from his land."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה עַתָּה תִרְאֶה אֲשֶׁר אֶעֱשֶׂה לְפַרְעֹה כִּי בְיָד חֲזָקָה יְשַׁלְּחֵם וּבְיָד חֲזָקָה יְגָרְשֵׁם מֵאַרְצוֹ (beyad khazaqah yeshalekhem u-veyad khazaqah yegareshem, "by a strong hand he will send them out and by a strong hand he will drive them out"). LXX (Vaticanus): ἤδη ὄψῃ ἃ ποιήσω τῷ Φαραω· ἐν γὰρ χειρὶ κραταιᾷ ἐξαποστελεῖ αὐτοὺς καὶ ἐν βραχίονι ὑψηλῷ ἐκβαλεῖ αὐτούς ("now you shall see what I will do to Pharao; for by a mighty hand he will send them out, and by a high arm he will cast them out") -- LXX has "high arm" (βραχίονι ὑψηλῷ) for the second "strong hand," creating a parallelism not in the MT.

**2** And God spoke to Mosheh, and he said to him: "I [am] YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר אֱלֹהִים אֶל־מֹשֶׁה וַיֹּאמֶר אֵלָיו אֲנִי יְהוָה (ani YHWH). LXX: ἐλάλησεν δὲ ὁ θεὸς πρὸς Μωυσῆν καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν Ἐγώ κύριος ("and God spoke to Moses and said to him: I [am] Lord") -- renders YHWH as κύριος.

**3** And I appeared to Avraham, to Yitschaq, and to Ya'aqov as El Shaddai [God Almighty], and [by] my name YHWH I was not known to them.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וָאֵרָא אֶל־אַבְרָהָם אֶל־יִצְחָק וְאֶל־יַעֲקֹב בְּאֵל שַׁדָּי וּשְׁמִי יְהוָה לֹא נוֹדַעְתִּי לָהֶם (u-shemi YHWH lo noda'ti lahem, "and my name YHWH I was not known to them"). The niphal noda'ti is first person: "I was not made known." This is the critical theological statement: the patriarchs knew God as El Shaddai (אֵל שַׁדָּי), but the name YHWH in its fullness of covenant-keeping meaning was not revealed to them. LXX: καὶ ὤφθην πρὸς Αβρααμ καὶ Ισαακ καὶ Ιακωβ θεὸς ὢν αὐτῶν, καὶ τὸ ὄνομά μου κύριος οὐκ ἐδήλωσα αὐτοῖς ("and I appeared to Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, being their God, and my name Lord I did not reveal to them") -- LXX adds θεὸς ὢν αὐτῶν ("being their God") and uses ἐδήλωσα ("I did not reveal," active) rather than the Hebrew niphal.

**4** And also I established my covenant with them, to give to them the land of Kena'an, the land of their sojourning, which they sojourned in it.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְגַם הֲקִמֹתִי אֶת־בְּרִיתִי אִתָּם לָתֵת לָהֶם אֶת־אֶרֶץ כְּנָעַן אֵת אֶרֶץ מְגֻרֵיהֶם אֲשֶׁר־גָּרוּ בָהּ (et erets megureihem asher-garu vah, "the land of their sojournings which they sojourned in it"). LXX: καὶ ἔστησα τὴν διαθήκην μου πρὸς αὐτούς ("and I established my covenant with them") -- renders berit as διαθήκην ("covenant/testament").

**5** And also I have heard the groaning of the sons of Yisra'el, whom Mitsrayim [are] enslaving them, and I remembered my covenant.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְגַם אֲנִי שָׁמַעְתִּי אֶת־נַאֲקַת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל אֲשֶׁר מִצְרַיִם מַעֲבִדִים אֹתָם (na'aqat, "groaning"; ma'avidim, hiphil participle, "enslaving/causing to serve"). וָאֶזְכֹּר אֶת־בְּרִיתִי (va'ezkor et-beriti, "and I remembered my covenant"). LXX: τὸν στεναγμὸν τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ ("the groaning of the sons of Israel").

**6** Therefore say to the sons of Yisra'el: 'I [am] YHWH, and I will bring you out from under the sivlot [burdens] of Mitsrayim, and I will deliver you from their labor, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great judgments.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לָכֵן אֱמֹר לִבְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל אֲנִי יְהוָה וְהוֹצֵאתִי אֶתְכֶם מִתַּחַת סִבְלֹת מִצְרַיִם וְהִצַּלְתִּי אֶתְכֶם מֵעֲבֹדָתָם וְגָאַלְתִּי אֶתְכֶם בִּזְרוֹעַ נְטוּיָה וּבִשְׁפָטִים גְּדֹלִים (vehotse'ti... vehitsalti... vega'alti... bizroa' netuyah u-vishfatim gedolim). The four-fold "I will" promise begins here: (1) "I will bring out" (hotse'ti, hiphil); (2) "I will deliver" (hitsalti, hiphil); (3) "I will redeem" (ga'alti) -- the verb ga'al implies kinsman-redeemer obligation. LXX: ἐξάξω... ῥύσομαι... λυτρώσομαι ἐν βραχίονι ὑψηλῷ καὶ κρίσει μεγάλῃ ("I will lead out... rescue... ransom with a high arm and great judgment").

**7** And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you for a God; and you shall know that I [am] YHWH your God, the one bringing you out from under the sivlot [burdens] of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתִּי אֶתְכֶם לִי לְעָם וְהָיִיתִי לָכֶם לֵאלֹהִים (velaqakhti etkhem li le'am vehayiti lakhem le'lohim). The fourth "I will": (4) "I will take" (laqakhti). וִידַעְתֶּם כִּי אֲנִי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם הַמּוֹצִיא אֶתְכֶם מִתַּחַת סִבְלוֹת מִצְרָיִם (ha-motsi, hiphil participle, "the one bringing out"). LXX: καὶ λήμψομαι ἐμαυτῷ ὑμᾶς λαὸν ἐμοὶ ("and I will take you to myself as a people for me").

**8** And I will bring you to the land which I lifted up my hand to give it to Avraham, to Yitschaq, and to Ya'aqov; and I will give it to you [as] a morashah [possession/inheritance]; I [am] YHWH.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהֵבֵאתִי אֶתְכֶם אֶל־הָאָרֶץ אֲשֶׁר נָשָׂאתִי אֶת־יָדִי (nasa'ti et-yadi, "I lifted my hand" -- an oath gesture) לָתֵת אֹתָהּ לְאַבְרָהָם לְיִצְחָק וּלְיַעֲקֹב וְנָתַתִּי אֹתָהּ לָכֶם מוֹרָשָׁה (morashah: permanent hereditary possession, from yarash, "to possess/inherit"). LXX: εἰς ἣν ἐξέτεινα τὴν χεῖρά μου δοῦναι αὐτήν ("to which I stretched out my hand to give it") -- renders the oath idiom similarly. κληρονομίαν ("as an inheritance") for morashah.

**9** And Mosheh spoke thus to the sons of Yisra'el, and they did not listen to Mosheh from qotser ruach [shortness of spirit] and from hard labor.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר מֹשֶׁה כֵּן אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְלֹא שָׁמְעוּ אֶל־מֹשֶׁה מִקֹּצֶר רוּחַ וּמֵעֲבֹדָה קָשָׁה (mi-qotser ruach u-me'avodah qashah). Qotser ruach (קֹצֶר רוּחַ): literally "shortness of spirit/breath" -- could denote anguish, impatience, or crushed spirit. LXX: ἀπὸ τῆς ὀλιγοψυχίας καὶ ἀπὸ τῶν ἔργων τῶν σκληρῶν ("from small-souledness and from the hard works") -- ὀλιγοψυχία ("small-soul") captures the Hebrew well.

**10** And YHWH spoke to Mosheh, saying:

**11** "Go, speak to Par'oh king of Mitsrayim, and let him send out the sons of Yisra'el from his land."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads בֹּא דַבֵּר אֶל־פַּרְעֹה מֶלֶךְ מִצְרָיִם וִישַׁלַּח אֶת־בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל מֵאַרְצוֹ (bo dabber, "go, speak"). LXX: εἴσελθε λάλησον Φαραω βασιλεῖ Αἰγύπτου ("enter, speak to Pharao king of Egypt").

**12** And Mosheh spoke before YHWH, saying: "Behold, the sons of Yisra'el did not listen to me, and how shall Par'oh listen to me? And I [am] of uncircumcised lips."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר מֹשֶׁה לִפְנֵי יְהוָה לֵאמֹר הֵן בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל לֹא־שָׁמְעוּ אֵלַי וְאֵיךְ יִשְׁמָעֵנִי פַרְעֹה וַאֲנִי עֲרַל שְׂפָתָיִם (va'ani aral sefatayim, "and I [am] uncircumcised of lips"). The phrase aral sefatayim (עֲרַל שְׂפָתָיִם) is a metaphor: lips that are "blocked" or "sealed," hence unable to speak fluently. LXX: καὶ πῶς εἰσακούσεταί μου Φαραω; ἐγὼ δὲ ἄλογός εἰμι ("and how shall Pharao listen to me? And I am without speech") -- renders aral sefatayim as ἄλογος ("speechless/without words").

**13** And YHWH spoke to Mosheh and to Aharon, and he commanded them to the sons of Yisra'el and to Par'oh king of Mitsrayim, to bring out the sons of Yisra'el from the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה וְאֶל־אַהֲרֹן וַיְצַוֵּם אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְאֶל־פַּרְעֹה מֶלֶךְ מִצְרָיִם לְהוֹצִיא אֶת־בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (le-hotsi, hiphil infinitive, "to bring out"). LXX: καὶ συνέταξεν αὐτοῖς... ὥστε ἐξαγαγεῖν ("and he commanded them... so as to lead out").

**14** These [are] the heads of the house of their fathers: the sons of Re'uven, firstborn of Yisra'el -- Khanokh and Pallu, and Chetsron and Karmi; these [are] the clans of Re'uven.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֵלֶּה רָאשֵׁי בֵית־אֲבֹתָם (rashei veit-avotam, "heads of the house of their fathers"). בְּנֵי רְאוּבֵן בְּכֹר יִשְׂרָאֵל חֲנוֹךְ וּפַלּוּא חֶצְרוֹן וְכַרְמִי (Khanokh, Pallu, Chetsron, Karmi). אֵלֶּה מִשְׁפְּחֹת רְאוּבֵן (mishpekhot, "clans/families"). LXX: Ενωχ καὶ Φαλλους, Ασρων καὶ Χαρμι ("Enoch and Phallous, Asron and Charmi").

**15** And the sons of Shim'on -- Yemu'el and Yamin and Ohad and Yakhin and Tsochar and Sha'ul the son of the Kena'anit [Canaanite woman]; these [are] the clans of Shim'on.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי שִׁמְעוֹן יְמוּאֵל וְיָמִין וְאֹהַד וְיָכִין וְצֹחַר וְשָׁאוּל בֶּן־הַכְּנַעֲנִית (ben-ha-Kena'anit, "son of the Canaanite woman"). LXX: Ιεμουηλ καὶ Ιαμιν καὶ Αωδ καὶ Ιαχιν καὶ Σααρ καὶ Σαουλ ὁ ἐκ τῆς Φοινίσσης ("Saoul the one from the Phoenician woman") -- renders "Canaanite" as "Phoenician."

**16** And these [are] the names of the sons of Levi according to their toledot [generations]: Gershon and Qehat and Merari. And the years of the life of Levi -- seven and thirty and a hundred years.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֵלֶּה שְׁמוֹת בְּנֵי־לֵוִי לְתֹלְדֹתָם גֵּרְשׁוֹן וּקְהָת וּמְרָרִי (Gershon, Qehat, Merari). וּשְׁנֵי חַיֵּי לֵוִי שֶׁבַע וּשְׁלֹשִׁים וּמְאַת שָׁנָה (sheva u-sheloshim u-me'at shanah, "seven and thirty and a hundred years" = 137 years). LXX: Γεδσων, Κααθ καὶ Μεραρι· καὶ τὰ ἔτη τῆς ζωῆς Λευι ἑκατὸν τριάκοντα ἑπτά ("and the years of the life of Levi: one hundred thirty-seven").

**17** The sons of Gershon -- Livni and Shim'i, according to their clans.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads בְּנֵי גֵרְשׁוֹן לִבְנִי וְשִׁמְעִי לְמִשְׁפְּחֹתָם (Livni ve-Shim'i le-mishpekhotam). LXX: Λοβενι καὶ Σεμεϊ ("Lobeni and Semei").

**18** And the sons of Qehat -- Amram and Yitshar and Chevron and Uzzi'el. And the years of the life of Qehat -- three and thirty and a hundred years.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי קְהָת עַמְרָם וְיִצְהָר וְחֶבְרוֹן וְעֻזִּיאֵל (Amram, Yitshar, Chevron, Uzzi'el). וּשְׁנֵי חַיֵּי קְהָת שָׁלֹשׁ וּשְׁלֹשִׁים וּמְאַת שָׁנָה (shalosh u-sheloshim u-me'at shanah = 133 years). LXX: Αμβραμ καὶ Ισσααρ, Χεβρων καὶ Οζιηλ· καὶ τὰ ἔτη τῆς ζωῆς Κααθ ἑκατὸν τριάκοντα τρία ("133 years").

**19** And the sons of Merari -- Machli and Mushi. These [are] the clans of Levi according to their toledot [generations].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי מְרָרִי מַחְלִי וּמוּשִׁי (Machli u-Mushi). אֵלֶּה מִשְׁפְּחֹת הַלֵּוִי לְתֹלְדֹתָם (mishpekhot ha-Levi le-toledotam). LXX: Μοολι καὶ Ομουσι ("Mooli and Omousi").

**20** And Amram took Yokheved his father's-sister to him for a wife, and she bore to him Aharon and Mosheh. And the years of the life of Amram -- seven and thirty and a hundred years.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקַּח עַמְרָם אֶת־יוֹכֶבֶד דֹּדָתוֹ לוֹ לְאִשָּׁה (Yokheved dodato, "Yokheved his aunt/father's-sister"). וַתֵּלֶד לוֹ אֶת־אַהֲרֹן וְאֶת־מֹשֶׁה (vateled lo et-Aharon ve'et-Mosheh). וּשְׁנֵי חַיֵּי עַמְרָם שֶׁבַע וּשְׁלֹשִׁים וּמְאַת שָׁנָה (137 years). LXX: ἔλαβεν τὴν Ιωχαβεδ θυγατέρα τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ ("he took Iochabed, daughter of the brother of his father") -- LXX reinterprets dodato ("his father's sister") as "daughter of his father's brother" (i.e., cousin), possibly to avoid the later Levitical prohibition (Lev 18:12).

**21** And the sons of Yitshar -- Qorach and Nefeg and Zikhri.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי יִצְהָר קֹרַח וָנֶפֶג וְזִכְרִי (Qorach, Nefeg, Zikhri). LXX: Κορε καὶ Ναφεκ καὶ Ζεχρι ("Kore and Naphek and Zechri").

**22** And the sons of Uzzi'el -- Misha'el and Eltsafan and Sitri.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי עֻזִּיאֵל מִישָׁאֵל וְאֶלְצָפָן וְסִתְרִי (Misha'el, Eltsafan, Sitri). LXX: Μισαηλ καὶ Ελισαφαν καὶ Σεθρι ("Misael and Elisaphan and Sethri").

**23** And Aharon took Elisheva daughter of Amminadav, sister of Nachshon, to him for a wife; and she bore to him Nadav and Avihu, El'azar and Itamar.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקַּח אַהֲרֹן אֶת־אֱלִישֶׁבַע בַּת־עַמִּינָדָב אֲחוֹת נַחְשׁוֹן לוֹ לְאִשָּׁה (Elisheva bat-Amminadav akhot Nachshon). וַתֵּלֶד לוֹ אֶת־נָדָב וְאֶת־אֲבִיהוּא אֶת־אֶלְעָזָר וְאֶת־אִיתָמָר (Nadav, Avihu, El'azar, Itamar). LXX: Ελισαβεθ θυγατέρα Αμιναδαβ ἀδελφὴν Ναασσων ("Elisabet daughter of Aminadab, sister of Naasson").

**24** And the sons of Qorach -- Assir and Elqanah and Avi'asaf; these [are] the clans of the Qorchi [Korahites].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְנֵי קֹרַח אַסִּיר וְאֶלְקָנָה וַאֲבִיאָסָף (Assir, Elqanah, Avi'asaf). אֵלֶּה מִשְׁפְּחֹת הַקָּרְחִי (ha-Qorkhi, "the Korahite"). LXX: Ασιρ καὶ Ελκανα καὶ Αβιασαφ ("Asir and Elkana and Abiasaph").

**25** And El'azar son of Aharon took to him from the daughters of Puti'el for a wife; and she bore to him Pinechas. These [are] the heads of the fathers of the Levi'im [Levites] according to their clans.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶלְעָזָר בֶּן־אַהֲרֹן לָקַח־לוֹ מִבְּנוֹת פּוּטִיאֵל לוֹ לְאִשָּׁה (mi-benot Puti'el, "from the daughters of Puti'el"). וַתֵּלֶד לוֹ אֶת־פִּינְחָס (Pinechas). אֵלֶּה רָאשֵׁי אֲבוֹת הַלְוִיִּם לְמִשְׁפְּחֹתָם (rashei avot ha-Levi'im le-mishpekhotam). LXX: τῶν θυγατέρων Φουτιηλ ("of the daughters of Phoutiel").

**26** He [is] Aharon and Mosheh, to whom YHWH said: "Bring out the sons of Yisra'el from the land of Mitsrayim according to their tsiv'ot [hosts/armies]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הוּא אַהֲרֹן וּמֹשֶׁה אֲשֶׁר אָמַר יְהוָה לָהֶם הוֹצִיאוּ אֶת־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם עַל־צִבְאֹתָם (al-tsiv'otam, "according to their hosts"). The order here is Aharon before Mosheh (reversed from the genealogical sequence), indicating Aharon's primacy in the priestly line. LXX: κατὰ συστήματα αὐτῶν ("according to their companies") for tsiv'ot.

**27** They [are] the ones speaking to Par'oh king of Mitsrayim to bring out the sons of Yisra'el from Mitsrayim; he [is] Mosheh and Aharon.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הֵם הַמְדַבְּרִים אֶל־פַּרְעֹה מֶלֶךְ־מִצְרַיִם (hem ha-medabberim, "they [are] the ones speaking" -- participle emphasizing ongoing role). הוּא מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן (hu Mosheh ve-Aharon). The order now reverses back to Mosheh before Aharon, emphasizing his role as speaker to Par'oh. LXX: οὗτός ἐστιν Ααρων καὶ Μωυσῆς -- note LXX keeps the Aharon-first order here, differing from MT.

**28** And it was on the day YHWH spoke to Mosheh in the land of Mitsrayim,

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְהִי בְּיוֹם דִּבֶּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (vayehi beyom dibber YHWH, "and it was on the day [that] YHWH spoke").

**29** and YHWH spoke to Mosheh, saying: "I [am] YHWH. Speak to Par'oh king of Mitsrayim all that I [am] speaking to you."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה לֵּאמֹר אֲנִי יְהוָה דַּבֵּר אֶל־פַּרְעֹה מֶלֶךְ מִצְרַיִם אֵת כָּל־אֲשֶׁר אֲנִי דֹּבֵר אֵלֶיךָ (et kol-asher ani dover elekha, "all that I [am] speaking to you"). LXX: ὅσα ἐγὼ λέγω σοι ("whatever I say to you").

**30** And Mosheh said before YHWH: "Behold, I [am] of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Par'oh listen to me?"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה לִפְנֵי יְהוָה הֵן אֲנִי עֲרַל שְׂפָתַיִם וְאֵיךְ יִשְׁמַע אֵלַי פַּרְעֹה (aral sefatayim, "uncircumcised of lips"). This repeats the objection of v.12, forming an inclusio that frames the genealogy (vv.14-25). LXX: ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἰσχνόφωνός εἰμι, καὶ πῶς εἰσακούσεταί μου Φαραω ("behold, I am weak-voiced, and how shall Pharao listen to me?") -- renders aral sefatayim as ἰσχνόφωνος ("weak/thin-voiced"), a different metaphor from ἄλογος in v.12.

---

## Key Terminology -- Exodus 6

| Hebrew | Transliteration | Literal Meaning | Verses |
|---|---|---|---|
| אֲנִי יְהוָה | ani YHWH | I [am] YHWH | 2, 6, 7, 8, 29 |
| אֵל שַׁדָּי | El Shaddai | God Almighty | 3 |
| לֹא נוֹדַעְתִּי | lo noda'ti | I was not known (niphal) | 3 |
| בְּרִית | berit | covenant | 4, 5 |
| נַאֲקַת | na'aqat | groaning | 5 |
| סִבְלֹת | sivlot | burdens/forced labor | 6, 7 |
| וְהוֹצֵאתִי | vehotse'ti | and I will bring out | 6 |
| וְהִצַּלְתִּי | vehitsalti | and I will deliver | 6 |
| וְגָאַלְתִּי | vega'alti | and I will redeem | 6 |
| וְלָקַחְתִּי | velaqakhti | and I will take | 7 |
| מוֹרָשָׁה | morashah | possession/inheritance | 8 |
| קֹצֶר רוּחַ | qotser ruach | shortness of spirit/breath | 9 |
| עֲרַל שְׂפָתַיִם | aral sefatayim | uncircumcised of lips | 12, 30 |
| דֹּדָתוֹ | dodato | his father's-sister/aunt | 20 |
| תּוֹלְדֹת | toledot | generations | 16, 19 |
| צִבְאֹת | tsiv'ot | hosts/armies | 26 |
| מִשְׁפְּחֹת | mishpekhot | clans/families | 14, 15, 17, 19, 24, 25 |

## Variant Notes Summary

| Verse | MT (Leningrad Codex) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | בְיָד חֲזָקָה... וּבְיָד חֲזָקָה ("by a strong hand... and by a strong hand") | ἐν χειρὶ κραταιᾷ... ἐν βραχίονι ὑψηλῷ ("by a mighty hand... by a high arm") | LXX creates parallelism with "high arm" for second clause |
| 3 | בְּאֵל שַׁדָּי ("as El Shaddai") | θεὸς ὢν αὐτῶν ("being their God") | LXX adds "being their God," paraphrases El Shaddai |
| 3 | לֹא נוֹדַעְתִּי ("I was not known," niphal) | οὐκ ἐδήλωσα ("I did not reveal," active) | LXX shifts from passive to active voice |
| 6 | בִּשְׁפָטִים גְּדֹלִים ("with great judgments," plural) | κρίσει μεγάλῃ ("with great judgment," singular) | LXX uses singular |
| 9 | מִקֹּצֶר רוּחַ ("from shortness of spirit") | ἀπὸ τῆς ὀλιγοψυχίας ("from small-souledness") | Excellent Greek calque |
| 12 | עֲרַל שְׂפָתָיִם ("uncircumcised of lips") | ἄλογός εἰμι ("I am without speech") | LXX avoids circumcision metaphor |
| 20 | דֹּדָתוֹ ("his father's-sister") | θυγατέρα τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ τοῦ πατρός ("daughter of his father's brother") | LXX changes aunt to cousin (avoids Lev 18:12 issue) |
| 26 | הוּא אַהֲרֹן וּמֹשֶׁה (Aharon then Mosheh) | Ααρων καὶ Μωυσῆς (same order) | Both preserve Aharon-first order |
| 27 | הוּא מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן (Mosheh then Aharon) | οὗτός ἐστιν Ααρων καὶ Μωυσῆς (Aharon then Mosheh) | LXX does NOT reverse to Mosheh-first as MT does |
| 30 | עֲרַל שְׂפָתַיִם ("uncircumcised of lips") | ἰσχνόφωνός εἰμι ("I am weak-voiced") | Different rendering from v.12 (ἄλογος); LXX varies its translation |
